title: Household surveys programme 2018–22 (third edition) | Stats NZ
content:
## Household surveys programme 2018–22 (third edition)
03 September 2018, 12:00pm
Household surveys programme 2018–22 (third edition) outlines our plans for a coordinated programme of supplementary content across our three household surveys: Household Labour Force Survey, New Zealand General Social Survey, and Household Economic Survey.
20 May 2019 : Update – we have decided to pause the HLFS supplement programme while we review how we collect supplementary information on our household surveys.
We are reviewing our household surveys supplement programme following some issues with supplementary content run on the Household Labour Force Survey (HLFS). These issues led to the Survey of Working Life (SoWL) 2018 being withdrawn from the field early and affected the quality of data from other supplementary topics on volunteer work and work-related health.
Consequently, we have decided to pause the HLFS supplement programme while we review how we collect supplementary information on our household surveys. We acknowledge there is a definite need for the supplementary data, both to meet customers’ information needs and international reporting requirements. However, we wish to review how we do this in order to ensure the quality of the data from the supplements and that they do not adversely affect the surveys to which they are attached.
The review will affect the schedule previously published in Household surveys supplement programme: 2018–22 (third edition) .
We are delaying the scheduled HLFS supplements on education and training, displaced workers, volunteer work, and employment transitions. We also need to consider what form SoWL should take in future, which may affect its scheduling. This will not affect supplementary topics which are already running successfully on rotating schedules on the HLFS, namely income and disability. At this stage, there are no planned changes to the programmes for the General Social Survey and Household Economic Survey.
We will update this page with the outcome of the review and publish a revised programme in due course. In the meantime, if you have any questions please contact:
Sean Broughton Manager, Labour Market and Household Statistics sean.broughton@stats.govt.nz 04 931 4344 or 021 714 553
- Employment and unemployment
